events.name: Tailor Abuses His Wife
events.description: There was once a tailor, who was a quarrelsome fellow, and his wife, who was good, industrious, and pious, never could please him. Whatever she did, he was not satisfied, but grumbled and scolded, and knocked her about and beat her.
thoughts: The event describes a situation of ongoing conflict between the tailor and his wife. The description does not indicate any resolution or closure on the issue, nor is it part of the main action driving the story forward. Instead, it sets up the problem that will likely escalate in future events.

events.name: Tailor Promises Better Behavior
events.description: As the authorities at last heard of it, they had the tailor summoned, and put in prison in order to make him better. The tailor was kept for a while on bread and water, and then set free again. The tailor was forced, however, to promise not to beat his wife any more, but to live with her in peace, and share joy and sorrow with her, as married people ought to do.
thoughts: The event marks a turning point where the authorities intervene, and the quarrelsome tailor is forced to promise change. This suggests a shift from conflict to resolution.

The event involves a resolution of the conflict between the quarrelsome tailor and his wife. The authorities intervene, and the tailor is forced to promise to stop beating his wife, indicating that the story has reached a point where the main issue is being addressed.

events.name: Tailor Chases His Wife
events.description: All went on well for a time, but then tailor fell into his old ways, and was surly and quarrelsome. And because tailor dared not beat her, he would seize her by the hair and tear it out. The woman escaped from him, and sprang out into the yard, but he ran after her with his yard-measure and scissors, and chased her about, and threw the yard-measure and scissors at her, and whatever else came his way.
thoughts: The event describes a continuation of the husband's abusive behavior towards his wife, escalating from physical violence to chasing her with household items. This aligns with option 0 as it is part of the main narrative action, driving the story forward through conflict.

events.name: Tailor Continues Abusive Behavior
events.description: When he hit her he laughed, and when he missed her, he stormed and swore. This went on so long that the neighbours came to the wife’s assistance. The tailor was again summoned before the magistrates, and reminded of his promise.
thoughts: The event involves the tailor acting out his violent behavior towards his wife again, which leads to external intervention from the neighbors and a reminder of his promise to change. This is not the main action driving the story forward or the final resolution, but rather an escalation of the conflict.

events.name: Judge Questions Tailor's Claim
events.description: “Dear gentlemen,” said he, “I have kept my word, I have not beaten her, but have shared joy and sorrow with her.” “How can that be,” said the judge, “when she continually brings such heavy complaints against you?”
thoughts: The event involves a confrontation between the tailor and the judge, where the judge questions the tailor's claim of sharing joy and sorrow with his wife. This is not part of the main action or conflict resolution, but rather a moment of reflection on past events.

events.name: Tailor Chases His Wife
events.description: “I have not beaten her, but just because she looked so strange I wanted to comb her hair with my hand; she, however, got away from me, and left me quite spitefully. Then I hurried after her, and in order to bring her back to her duty, I threw at her as a well-meant admonition whatever came readily to hand.”
